channel.title: Comments for page "Etymology of die Glocke"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Etymology of die Glocke"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the things that I really like about works like this is that they let me know about good SCPs that I haven't seen yet.
By some coincidence, I was reading recently about Germany's collapse at the end of WWII. One unexpected detail was the motivation of German soldiers at the end; except for a few fanatics, it wasn't about the Fuhrer or the Party, it was about trying to save their country from a devastating defeat. I saw some of that in this; his concern for his wife and child felt sincere.

The idea that he 'disappeared' and she never knew what happened is genuinely moving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda feel sorry for the Nazi and his family.

+ show block
– hide block

While writing this, I thought of two endings (factoring in that 2367 was activated in the end). Either the Webers reunited and everyone travelled through time or Ursula el al. did not arrive and Otto was abandoned/killed/forcefully dragged into die Glocke by his colleagues.

+1 for a good story and making me confused on how to feel about a Nazi.

Indeed. There is a tendency to paint certain groups with a broad stroke, simply due to various atrocities committed by members of said groups. But groups of humans are not monolithic, and I would like to believe that people are still people in the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda feel sorry for the Nazi and his family. Then again, the bathos is also humorous.

+1 for a good story and making me confused on how to feel about a Nazi.

item.title: 2016 X-mas Art Exchange, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
This is originally a X-mas Art Exchange (2016) for Agent MacLeod. He was pleased with it and desired to have it posted. I made significant edits to stretch out the letters (the original version was a single letter with the content feeling a bit congested, as I ultimately evaluated).

This article features the following articles:

  • keine Kosten zu hoch by Agent MacLeod
  • SCP-044 (World War II Era Molecular-Fission Cannon) by FritzWillie
  • SCP-2367 (Splatzshtaffel) by Agent MacLeod

Link to the original version is here, for interested parties.

As to why I took this approach when MacLeod asked for things about his characters, I wanted to do arc welding for the two articles that use Weber and make him more than just a Nazi scientist (a archetype that is prone to sweeping assumptions).
